title: I Forgot
lyrics:
[verse 1]
My bowl tipped over—*splat!*—what a mess!
The bus honks loud, I’m in distress.
The form’s not signed, it’s lost, it’s gone.
I can’t find socks, my shoe’s undone!

[chorus]
I forgot, I forgot!
It’s not my fault—it’s just a lot!
I forgot, I forgot!
Now my brain’s in a knot!

[verse 2]
The homework’s crumpled, wet with tears.
The clock goes *tick-tock*—no time to spare!
My backpack’s empty, my shirt’s inside-out.
Mom’s sighing loud—what’s all the shout?

[chorus]
I forgot, I forgot!
It’s not my fault—it’s just a lot!
I forgot, I forgot!
Now my brain’s in a knot!

[ending]
I forgot… I forgot…
Maybe I’ll just nap a lot…
style_prompt: Catchy children's song, guitar and beatbox, 109 BPM, strong bass, punchy beat, bouncy groovy infectious singalong, impossible to sit still, makes you move
scene: The kitchen clock reads 8:17 AM, and the bus will honk in three minutes—Mom just snapped, *"Where’s your permission slip for the field trip?"* as you stare at the crumpled homework pile on the counter, your cereal bowl still half-full. Your backpack gapes open, the signed form nowhere in sight, and your stomach drops like the milk carton you just knocked over.
cover_description: A kid in pajamas staring at a spilled cereal bowl while a bus honks outside.
